name: Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular
name_original: Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior desde Becerreá — circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Becerreá are set within the mountainous terrain of the Os Ancares Biosphere Reserve, a region characterized by deep valleys, river formations, and extensive forests. The landscape features significant elevation changes, making it suitable for challenging mountain biking. The area is carved by the upper course of the Navia River and its tributaries, providing varied topography. Forests of oak, birch, and holm oak contribute to the diverse riding environments. Best mountain bike trails around Becerreá The most popular mountain bike trail is Os Mazos and Neira de Rei from Baralla, an 18.1 mile (29.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete, offering scenic views along the Nevia River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia, a difficult 19.3 mile (31.1 km) path that explores the varied topography of the Navia River basin. Local mountain bikers also love the Hiking from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais - circular route through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, a 22.8 mile (36.7 km) trail leading through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Becerreá is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and extensive forests, including ancient Roman roads. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Becerreá are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Becerreá's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Becerreá?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Becerreá, offering a variety of routes through the region's mountainous terrain and river valleys. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 50 times.

Q: What are the general characteristics of mountain biking trails in Becerreá?
A: Mountain biking in Becerreá is defined by its mountainous terrain, significant elevation changes, and diverse landscapes, including river valleys and extensive forests of oak, birch, and holm oak. Many routes follow ancient Roman roads, providing a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Becerreá for beginners or families?
A: The mountain bike trails around Becerreá are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no routes specifically listed as easy. The region's mountainous terrain means most trails involve significant elevation changes, making them more suitable for riders with some experience. For a moderate option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211375416/as-nogais-circular-route-along-the-navia-river-cruzul-agueeira-ecological-conservation-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.4 km long with 440m of elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in the Becerreá area?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Becerreá offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210606295/os-mazos-and-neira-de-rei-from-baralla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Os Mazos and Neira de Rei from Baralla&lt;/a&gt; trail is a demanding 29.2 km route with over 1000m of elevation gain. Another difficult option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210654475/paradela-y-penamil-desde-a-pobra-circular-del-rio-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.1 km path exploring varied topography along the Navia River.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Becerreá?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Becerreá are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210569136/mosteiro-de-santa-maria-de-penamaior-from-becerrea-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.4 km long, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211375416/as-nogais-circular-route-along-the-navia-river-cruzul-agueeira-ecological-conservation-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails in Becerreá?
A: The trails in Becerreá offer diverse and picturesque scenery, as the region is part of the Os Ancares Biosphere Reserve. You'll ride through deep valleys, along river formations like the Navia River, and through extensive forests of oak, birch, and holm oak. The landscape changes vibrantly with the seasons, providing a beautiful backdrop for your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Becerreá?
A: The mountain bike routes in Becerreá are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Os Ancares Biosphere Reserve, and the varied riding environments found along the trails.

Q: Are there any historical or natural attractions to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and natural beauty. You might encounter ancient Roman roads that form part of the trails. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt;, an over 800-year-old natural monument, and sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4750127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through the wider region near Triacastela.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Becerreá?
A: The diverse forests of Becerreá offer a picturesque backdrop that changes with the seasons. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the autumnal landscape, with its colorful deciduous forests, is particularly striking. Spring and early summer also provide pleasant conditions before the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through protected areas?
A: Yes, some routes lead through protected natural areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211428925/hiking-from-teso-da-lama-figueiro-y-becerrea-to-as-nogais-circular-route-through-the-special-conservation-area-cruzul-agueeira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, offering a chance to experience the well-preserved mountain ecosystem of the Os Ancares Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain in Becerreá is varied, ranging from shaded forest tracks to open, sweeping views across grasslands. You'll encounter significant elevation changes due to the mountainous landscape, with trails carved by river valleys and often utilizing historical pathways like old Roman roads.
tips: Becerreá sits in a gorge in this mountainous region on the Jacobean route between Madrid and Lugo. Good place to start your adventure in the area.
A small Monastery of Santa María de Penamaior in this mountain range of Lugo.
